 The Planck Gate & TRRS Singularity Reclamation

Project Overview: "The Black Hole Explained"

This Project provides the visual and logical framework for the Terminal Radiative Reclamation System (TRRS). It demonstrates how the "Singularity" is not a point of infinite density, but a functional mechanical gateway—the Planck Gate—where matter is processed, inverted, and reclaimed.

The Hypothesis: Beyond the Event Horizon

In standard General Relativity, the singularity is where the math breaks. I suggest that the math only breaks because we are missing the mechanical "Valve." By applying the geometry of the Planck Gate, we observe:

The Inversion Point: As matter reaches the Planck threshold, it undergoes a geometric flip.

TRRS (Terminal Radiative Reclamation System): The black hole acts as a cosmic engine, stripping information/matter to its fundamental state and cycling it back through the "Gate."

Mechanical Equilibrium: The singularity is redefined as a high-velocity throughput point rather than a static dead-end.

The Simulation & Documentation Simulation Dashboard: Planck_Gate_Singularity_Black_hole_V1_0.html (Interactive visual representation of system logic).

Instructions: Simply drag and drop this HTML file into any web browser's address bar. The simulation runs entirely within your local machine’s environment—no internet connection or external dependencies are required. It is a "sandboxed" execution, meaning it is safe, does not modify your computer, and can be stopped instantly by closing the browser tab.

Based on my Primary Research Paper:
